texto: O happy wound, full of delight, He whom You wound Is joyous indeed.
explicacao: Citação de Jacopone da Todi sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “O happy wound, full of delight, He whom You wound Is joyous indeed.” articula felicidade, joy, love em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como love.

seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Jacopone da Todi sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “O happy wound, full of delight, He whom You wound Is joyous indeed.” articula felicidade, joy,…

frase_original: O happy wound, full of delight, He whom You wound Is joyous indeed.

texto: What happens to the drop of wineThat you pour into the sea?Does it remain itself, unchanged?It is as if it never existed.So it is with the soul: Love drinks it in,It is united with Truth,Its old nature fades away,It is no longer master of itself.The soul wills and yet does not will:Its will belongs to Another.It has eyes only for this beauty;It no longer seeks to possess, as was its wont--It lacks the strength to possess such sweetness.The base of this highest of peaksIs founded on nichil,Shaped nothingness, made one with the Lord.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jacopone da Todi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “What happens to the drop of wineThat you pour into the sea?Does it remain itself, unchanged?It is…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, emptiness, light pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e light.
